---
title: "RNA-seq pipeline — example report"
author: "snakemake-rna-seq"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
jupyter: python3
---

This is a minimal example of a user-supplied [Quarto](https://quarto.org) report.
The pipeline renders every `*.qmd` it finds under the configured `reports_dir`
**last**, after all other steps finish, so a report can read any pipeline output.

Copy this folder, rename it (the folder and the `.qmd` must share a name, e.g.
`my_report/my_report.qmd`), and edit the cells below to summarise your own
results. The rendered HTML lands at
`<results_dir>/reports/<name>/<name>.html`.

## A self-contained figure

The cell below has no external dependencies, so this report always renders even
before the pipeline has produced any results.

```{python}
import plotly.express as px

fig = px.bar(
    x=["fastp", "FastQC", "RSEM/STAR", "DESeq2", "GSVA", "GSEA"],
    y=[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6],
    labels={"x": "pipeline step", "y": "example value"},
    title="Replace me with a plot of your own results",
)
fig.update_layout(template="plotly_white", showlegend=False)
fig
```

## Reading a real pipeline output

Uncomment and adjust the cell below to load one of the DESeq2 contrast tables and
show the top differentially expressed genes. Paths are relative to the directory
this `.qmd` lives in, so adjust the number of `../` to reach your `results_dir`
(here it points back up to the project root, then into `results/`).

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd

# Edit this to point at one of your contrasts, e.g.
# results/013_deseq2_gene_symbols/<treatment>_vs_<control>.csv
contrast_csv = Path("../../../results/013_deseq2_gene_symbols")

if contrast_csv.is_dir():
    csvs = sorted(contrast_csv.glob("*.csv"))
    if csvs:
        df = pd.read_csv(csvs[0])
        print(f"Top genes from {csvs[0].name}:")
        display(df.head(10))
    else:
        print(f"No result CSVs found yet in {contrast_csv}.")
else:
    print(
        "Results not found — run the pipeline first, then re-render. "
        f"(looked in {contrast_csv})"
    )
```
